mission.title: rook: CephPGImbalance Prometheus Alert doesn't account for device differences
mission.description: CephPGImbalance Prometheus Alert doesn't account for device differences. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify rook troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your rook deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
The `CephPGImbalance` alert fires in our cluster for every nvme OSD because they have fewer PGs than the "average" OSD.  Aren't there a variety of reasons an OSD would have fewer or more PGs than average?  Device Class...

steps.title: Apply the fix for CephPGImbalance Prometheus Alert doesn't account for device…
steps.description:
Any suggestion on how to fix this query? Or shall we just remove the alert completely? Based on the comments, maybe we just remove the alert for now. 

This originally was defined in the [Ceph repo](https://github.com/ceph/ceph/blob/main/monitoring/ceph-mixin/prometheus_alerts.yml#L236C17-L249), so
```yaml
abs(
 ((ceph_osd_numpg * on (ceph_daemon) group_left(device_class) ceph_osd_metadata > 0) - on (job, device_class) group_left avg(ceph_osd_numpg * on (ceph_daemon) group_left(device_class) ceph_osd_metadata > 0) by (job, device_class)) /
 on (job, device_class) group_left avg(ceph_osd_numpg * on (ceph_daemon) group_left(device_class) ceph_osd_metadata > 0) by (job, device_class)
) * on (ceph_daemon) group_left(hostname, device_class) ceph_osd_metadata > 0.30
```

steps.title: Confirm CephPGImbalance Prometheus Alert doesn't account… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Any suggestion on how to fix this query? Or shall we just remove the alert completely? Based on the comments, maybe we just remove the alert for now. 

This originally was defined in the [Ceph repo](https://github.com/ceph/ceph/blob/main/monitoring/ceph-mixin/prometheus_alerts.yml#L236C17-L249), so it's surprising the alert is still defined in the same form there.
codeSnippets:
abs(
 ((ceph_osd_numpg * on (ceph_daemon) group_left(device_class) ceph_osd_metadata > 0) - on (job, device_class) group_left avg(ceph_osd_numpg * on (ceph_daemon) group_left(device_class) ceph_osd_metadata > 0) by (job, device_class)) /
 on (job, device_class) group_left avg(ceph_osd_numpg * on (ceph_daemon) group_left(device_class) ceph_osd_metadata > 0) by (job, device_class)
) * on (ceph_daemon) group_left(hostname, device_class) ceph_osd_metadata > 0.30

abs(
  ((ceph_osd_numpg > 0) / ceph_osd_stat_bytes) * 
  on (job) group_left max(ceph_osd_stat_bytes) by (job)
)

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with rook installed or the issue environment reproducible.
